description: Add Microsoft provider card to Settings -> Integrations -> Providers.
Create `getMicrosoftIntegrationStatus` action returning masked status plus derived redirect URI/scope metadata.
Create `saveMicrosoftIntegrationSettings` action with validation for client ID, client secret, and optional tenant ID.
Create `resetMicrosoftProvidersToDisconnected` action to clear Microsoft provider connection state/tokens for tenant providers.
Export new Microsoft integration actions from integrations action entrypoints used by settings UI.
Persist `microsoft_client_id` as tenant secret from Providers settings.
Persist `microsoft_client_secret` as tenant secret from Providers settings.
Persist `microsoft_tenant_id` as tenant secret with default `common` when omitted.
Render masked saved-secret indicators in Microsoft settings status response/UI.
Enforce RBAC (`system_settings:update`) on Microsoft settings save/reset actions.
Restrict Microsoft settings actions from client portal user context.
Add provider-readiness check helper for Microsoft (`microsoft_client_id` + `microsoft_client_secret`).
Add provider-readiness check helper for Google (`google_client_id` + `google_client_secret`) for MSP SSO usage.
Update CE Microsoft email form to remove required manual client ID/client secret inputs.
Update CE Microsoft email form to display 'Configure Providers first' CTA when Microsoft provider settings are missing.
Update CE Microsoft calendar form to use provider-settings-first behavior and CTA when Microsoft settings are missing.
Ensure CE Microsoft email provider persistence no longer depends on per-provider client credential form fields.
Ensure CE Microsoft calendar provider persistence no longer depends on per-provider client credential form fields.
Replace CE SSO button stub with MSP-login-capable SSO buttons component.
Require non-empty email before enabling MSP SSO buttons.
Wire Microsoft MSP button to resolver + NextAuth OAuth flow.
Wire Google MSP button to resolver + NextAuth OAuth flow.
Show one generic resolver/start failure message across all failure reasons.
Keep client portal login SSO behavior unchanged (no new SSO buttons/flow).
Add unauthenticated `POST /api/auth/msp/sso/resolve` endpoint.
Validate/normalize resolver input (`provider`, `email`, `callbackUrl`) and reject malformed requests generically.
Resolver performs internal-user lookup by normalized email for source selection only.
Resolver chooses tenant source when user exists and tenant provider is configured for selected provider.
Resolver chooses app fallback source when tenant source is not available but app fallback keys are available.
Resolver applies same external behavior for unknown user as known-user-missing-provider paths.
Resolver returns identical response schema and status across lookup outcomes.
Resolver sets signed, short-lived httpOnly context cookie with source metadata.
Resolver context cookie excludes raw client IDs/secrets.
Resolver uses app fallback keys `MICROSOFT_OAUTH_*` for Microsoft fallback source.
Resolver uses app fallback keys `GOOGLE_OAUTH_*` for Google fallback source.
Resolver returns generic failure when neither tenant source nor app fallback is available.
Add basic resolver endpoint rate limiting keyed by request IP + normalized email hash bucket.
Add sanitized structured logs for resolver decisions/failures without user-existence leakage.
Allow CE MSP OAuth provider registration by removing effective EE-only gating for Google/Microsoft in NextAuth options path.
Refactor auth options caching so OAuth provider credentials can be resolved per request (remove/alter static cache).
Update OAuth secret resolution to read resolver context cookie and select tenant source when requested and valid.
When resolver context is absent/invalid/expired, fall back to app-level OAuth keys only.
Validate resolver cookie signature and TTL before applying tenant selection.
Overwrite resolver cookie on each new start attempt with short TTL to minimize stale context.
Add CE-safe OAuth profile mapping helper for MSP internal users (Google + Microsoft) in auth package.
Use CE-safe mapper in provider profile callbacks when edition is community.
Preserve existing EE registry-based mapper path when edition is enterprise.
Ensure CE MSP OAuth sign-in path does not require EE-only account-link persistence to succeed.
Ensure Microsoft tenant ID selection for OAuth issuer/authorization defaults to `common` when tenant value is empty.
Document in code comments that resolver lookup result must never be surfaced to client-facing messages.
Update `.env.example` comments to indicate `GOOGLE_OAUTH_*` and `MICROSOFT_OAUTH_*` can be used for CE MSP SSO fallback.
Add/update developer docs for provider setup order: Providers settings first, then Microsoft/Google integration-level connection.
